output:
Successfully executed T1, T2, T3.

T1: Changed citation URL format at line 4348 from `[N] {url}` to `[N] <{url}>` to suppress Discord same-channel message-link collapse. The fallback path `[N] {sid}` for missing metadata is preserved unchanged.

T2: Added URL validation guard at the attachment URL resolution point. Now prefers `proxy_url` when `url` does not start with 'http', falling back to bare `url` when both are bad (preserving existing fail-visible behavior). Added a top-of-function documentation comment in `_dispatch_understand_media` explaining the truncation investigation finding: archive data may store bare filenames in the `url` field, the guard mitigates this, and the new `media_url:` surface line in rejection output (to be added in T4) makes the actual URL visible in production.

T3: Enriched all four error-return sites in `_dispatch_understand_media` with source metadata:
  - (a) message_id not found (:1307-1313): added `message_id` as string.
  - (b) attachment_index out of range (:1318-1329): added `message_id`, `channel_id`, `guild_id` (from resolved source).
  - (c) no-url-field (:1343-1351): added `message_id`, `channel_id`, `guild_id` (from resolved source).
  - (d) download failure (:1379-1390): added `message_id`, `channel_id` (from source), `guild_id` (from source with context fallback), and `media_url`.
All fields are optional — only included when source data is available. `message_id` is cast to string; `channel_id`/`guild_id` kept as int-or-None.

Test results: 95 passed (64 runtime + 31 core), 2 expected failures (citation format assertions at runtime.py:3123 and core.py:358 — will be updated in T5).
deviations: Baseline test run captured 0 failures. Post-T1, 2 tests fail: runtime.py:3123 and core.py:358 — both assert on the old non-angle-bracket citation format. These are expected and will be fixed in T5 (next batch). All other 95 tests pass.
Advisory quality: code changes lacked test updates: src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py.

task_updates.executor_notes: Changed line 4348 from `f"[{idx}] {url}"` to `f"[{idx}] <{url}>"`. Fallback path `f"[{idx}] {sid}"` unchanged. Verified: 95 tests pass, 2 pre-identified citation format assertions fail (expected — fixed in T5).

Replaced `media_url = attachment.get('url') or attachment.get('proxy_url') or ''` with guard that prefers proxy_url when url doesn't start with 'http'. Falls back to bare url when both are bad. Added investigation-documentation comment block in function docstring. Verified: syntax check passes, no regression in non-citation tests.

Enriched all four error-return sites: (a) message_id-not-found adds message_id; (b) attachment_index out-of-range adds message_id/channel_id/guild_id from source; (c) no-url-field adds message_id/channel_id/guild_id from source; (d) download-failure adds message_id/channel_id/guild_id/media_url with guild_id context fallback. All fields optional/cast correctly. Verified: syntax check passes.

sense_check_acknowledgments.question: Does line 4312 now emit `[N] <url>` instead of `[N] url`? Is the fallback path `[N] {sid}` for missing metadata preserved unchanged?
sense_check_acknowledgments.verdict: PASS. Line 4348 now emits `f"[{idx}] <{url}>"`. The fallback path at line 4350 `f"[{idx}] {sid}"` is unchanged. Test failures confirm the new format is active — both failing tests show `<https://...>` in assertion output.

sense_check_acknowledgments.question: Does the URL validation guard correctly prefer `proxy_url` when `url` does not start with `http`? Does it fall back to the bare URL when both are bad (preserving the existing behavior of letting the download fail with a visible URL)? Is the documentation comment placed before the function body explaining the truncation finding?
sense_check_acknowledgments.verdict: PASS. Guard at lines 1334-1341: `_raw_url.startswith('http')` → use `_raw_url`; elif `_proxy_url` → use `_proxy_url`; else → use `_raw_url` (bare filename, let download fail visibly). Documentation comment is in the function docstring at line 1264 as a `.. note::` block explaining the truncation investigation finding.

sense_check_acknowledgments.question: Are all three error-return sites enriched? (out-of-range at :1303, no-url at :1315, download-failure at :1348). Does each only add fields when the source data is available? Is `message_id` cast to string and `channel_id`/`guild_id` kept as int-or-None?
sense_check_acknowledgments.verdict: PASS. Four error-return sites enriched (including message_id-not-found at :1307): (a) :1307-1313 adds message_id as string; (b) :1318-1329 adds message_id (str), channel_id/guild_id from source.get(); (c) :1343-1351 adds message_id (str), channel_id/guild_id from source.get(); (d) :1379-1390 adds message_id (str), channel_id from source, guild_id from source with context fallback, and media_url. All fields are conditional — only added when source data available. message_id cast via `str(message_id) if message_id is not None else None`; channel_id/guild_id as `source.get('channel_id')` — raw int-or-None from source dict.
